revision.comment: Undid revision 1196700145 by [[Special:Contributions/Shkuru Afshar|Shkuru Afshar]] ([[User talk:Shkuru Afshar|talk]]). unexplained, removed doc, no edit summary.
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported from [[:wikipedia:Template:UF-hcard-geo]]: importing template for usage on this wiki. It is used a lot
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Category:Template" to "Category:"
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:s with coordinates fields]] [[Category:s generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Undid revision 1196700145 by [[Special:Contributions/Shkuru Afshar|Shkuru Afshar]] ([[User talk:Shkuru Afshar|talk]]). unexplained, removed doc, no edit summary.
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:

revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[:en:Microformat|Microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[:en:Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[:en:Microformat|Microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[:en:Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[:en:Microformat|Microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[:en:Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[:en:Microformat|Microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[:en:HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[:en:Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[:en:Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[:en:Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[:en:Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[:en:Microformat|Microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Undid revision 1196700145 by [[Special:Contributions/Shkuru Afshar|Shkuru Afshar]] ([[User talk:Shkuru Afshar|talk]]). unexplained, removed doc, no edit summary.
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting this template since it seems broken in how it treats unknown parameters in [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Undid revision 1196700145 by [[Special:Contributions/Shkuru Afshar|Shkuru Afshar]] ([[User talk:Shkuru Afshar|talk]]). unexplained, removed doc, no edit summary.
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23631271398|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}}</noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "</noinclude>↵<noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
The HTML markup produced by this template includes an [[HCard| hCard microformat]], which makes the place-name and location [[Parsing|parsable]] by computers, either acting automatically to catalogue articles across Wikipedia, or via a browser tool operated by a person, to (for example) add the subject to an address book. Within the hCard is a [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformat]], which additionally makes the [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] (latitude & longitude) parsable, so that they can be, say, looked up on a map, or downloaded to a [[Global Positioning System|GPS]] unit. For more information about the use of [[microformat]]s on Wikipedia, please see [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats|the microformat project]].
=== Sub-templates === If the place or venue has "established", "founded", "opened" or similar dates, use {{tl|start date}} for the earliest of those dates [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Microformats/dates|unless the date is before 1583 CE]].
If it has a URL, use {{tl|URL}}.
'''Please do not remove instances of these sub-templates.'''
=== Classes === hCard uses HTML classes including: {{Flatlist| *adr *agent *category *county-name *extended-address *fn *label *locality *nickname *note *org *region *street-address *uid *url *vcard }}
Geo is produced by calling {{tl|coord}}, and uses HTML classes:
{{Flatlist| *geo *latitude *longitude }}
'''Please do not rename or remove these classes nor collapse nested elements which use them.'''
=== Precision === When giving coordinates, '''please use an [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Precision|appropriate level of precision]]'''. Do '''not''' use {{tl|coord}}'s {{para|name}} parameter.<includeonly>{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|doc | | }}</includeonly><noinclude>__NOTOC__{{doc}} [[Category:Templates with coordinates fields]] [[Category:Templates generating hCards and Geo]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
